feature_identity.feature_name: Mode C migration for remaining service-role integration tests

artifacts: lib/testing/create-mode-c-session.ts (auth-only ceremony helper, Phase B prerequisite)
in_scope_targets: transitive auth-path coverage for rpc_get_visit_loyalty_summary and rpc_get_visit_last_segment via Mode C tests of parent RPCs (rpc_get_player_recent_sessions, rpc_get_player_last_session_context)
out_of_scope_targets: Phase A mechanical compliance work (directives, gates, scripts) — treat as prerequisite
direct PostgREST tests of internal SQL composition RPCs with no TypeScript callers
unrelated product repairs except blocker capture

workflow: Select one bounded context or infra surface
Apply Phase A prerequisites if missing
Rewrite business-logic tests from service-role path to authenticated Mode C path
Close slice or mark formally blocked
acceptance_invariants: Fixture-only service_role use remains allowed — machine-verifiable: service_role client only in beforeAll/afterAll/setup*/cleanup*/create*Fixture scopes
Assertions are not weakened to force green
Auth-path, RLS, and grant failures become observable
Per-context execution remains serialized in Phase B
Skip ratio gate: if >20% of a context's tests are skipped due to non-auth blockers, context migration is BLOCKED not complete
SUPABASE_URL must resolve to 127.0.0.1 or localhost before Phase B execution

execution_environment.supabase_runtime: real running Supabase instance required for Phase B
execution_environment.collision_risk: shared fixture namespace and mutable database state

touch_surfaces: root test infrastructure surfaces where upstream pattern leverage exists
where.canonical_file_list: canonical-file-list.md — 17 Phase B targets (9 service-role-biz, 3 legacy-context, 3 mixed, 2 assess-first), 19 already Mode C, 25 misnamed non-integration tests

actors.responsibility: migrate one bounded slice to Mode C
validate conformance to posture and exclusions
run the migrated slice and surface failures

phase_order: Phase A before Phase B where prerequisite posture is missing
one context at a time for Phase B
when.completion_event: targeted context or infra slice reaches acceptance criteria or is formally blocked

why.operator_problem: service-role business-logic tests can pass while real authenticated paths are broken
pilot_value: converts hidden auth-path failures into visible failures
supports bounded hardening without product-surface churn
anti_goals: do not turn this into general testing modernization
do not accept counterfeit green tests

containment_loop.entry_condition: target slice still executes business logic through service_role or lacks direct auth-path coverage at known seams
loop_steps: close or escalate per expansion rule
containment_loop.exit_condition: acceptance criteria met or blocker formally recorded

required_outcomes: business-logic execution path migrated to Mode C
service_role confined to fixtures only (machine-verifiable: service_role client only in beforeAll/afterAll/setup*/cleanup* scopes)
known uncovered RPC seams (rpc_get_visit_loyalty_summary, rpc_get_visit_last_segment) exercised transitively via Mode C tests of parent RPCs
blockers recorded with class and owning context
artifact set stable enough for EXEC spec generation
canonical file list produced with exact file paths, current auth mode, target auth mode, and estimated complexity per file
explicit_exclusions: Phase A mechanical compliance work (directives, gates, scripts) — prerequisite not migration scope
direct PostgREST tests of internal SQL composition RPCs with no TypeScript callers

adjacent_rejected_ideas.idea: migrate all contexts in one sweep
treat infra surfaces as separate from the migration contract
adjacent_rejected_ideas.reason: helper and root surfaces may be upstream leverage points

adjacent_rejected_ideas.idea: promote existing getTestAuthenticatedClient() (auth + domain fixtures bundled) to shared location
adjacent_rejected_ideas.reason: 30 existing Mode C tests prove fixture shapes diverge per context — bundling auth + fixtures creates coupling where fixture schema changes break all importers simultaneously. Auth ceremony is the only truly duplicated piece (~20 lines).

adjacent_rejected_ideas.idea: keep auth setup inline everywhere with no shared helper
adjacent_rejected_ideas.reason: the auth ceremony is mechanical and identical across all tests — a thin helper eliminates the one piece that is genuinely duplicated without forcing fixture opinions

hard_dependencies: auth-only ceremony helper (lib/testing/create-mode-c-session.ts) — zero domain fixtures, caller-provided identity claims, static Bearer token, local-only safety assertion. Must be implemented and merged before Phase B. See FIB-H Section N.
running local Supabase instance (SUPABASE_URL must be 127.0.0.1 or localhost)
existing governance that defines Mode C as canonical
assumptions: current context synthesis is materially correct
Phase A precedes Phase B where needed
Phase B must be serialized per context

source_assertions.claim: Mode C uses authenticated anon client for business logic and service-role only for fixtures
Phase A is mechanical and Phase B requires running Supabase
Phase B should proceed one context at a time due to collisions

rpc_get_visit_loyalty_summary and rpc_get_visit_last_segment are uncovered seams — DA review corrected: these are internal SQL composition functions with zero TypeScript callers, exercised transitively via parent RPCs

recommended_exec_notes: produce exact canonical file list before generating tasks — no approximate counts
separate scope counts into target files, touch files, and infra surfaces
treat estimates as Phase B rewrite-only baseline excluding Phase A and defect tail
preserve serialized per-context execution in plan structure
surface parent RPCs (rpc_get_player_recent_sessions, rpc_get_player_last_session_context) as transitive coverage targets, not the internal child RPCs
implement auth-only ceremony helper (lib/testing/create-mode-c-session.ts) as first EXEC task — zero domain fixtures, static Bearer, local-only safety — must merge before any Phase B context work

expansion_trigger_rule: helper or infra bottleneck proves systemic
blocker volume reveals systemic auth/RLS flaw
governance promotes Mode C conformance to mandatory gated policy
